assessmentName: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server Production Optimization

principles: Discover server properties, supported metrics, SKU capabilities, cost, and prices dynamically before applying rules.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary current-cost signal.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for target compute, storage, backup, reserved capacity, and region price comparison.
Evaluate PostgreSQL at server, database, storage, connection, replication, and autovacuum health levels when metrics are available.
Prefer the best valid production target: right-size compute, tune storage, enable HA, tune connection pooling, fix autovacuum or bloat, improve replica posture, reserve stable capacity, or secure.
Never recommend a cheaper target that violates CPU, memory, storage, IOPS, connections, latency, HA, backup, replication, version, extension, or security requirements.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect PostgreSQL server properties, metrics, cost, prices, and security posture with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: Use subscription or resource-group list APIs instead of one GET per server.
Cache metric definitions by resource type, location, namespace, and API version.
Fetch multiple PostgreSQL metric names in one Azure Monitor metrics request using comma-separated metricnames.
Use database and server metric dimensions to identify which databases need deeper inspection.
Use Cost Management grouped by ResourceId once per scope instead of querying cost per server.
Use Retail Prices once per region and PostgreSQL meter group, cache paged results, and reuse for all servers in that region.
Fetch configurations, firewall rules, private endpoint details, or replica details only when a recommendation requires them.

minimumCallFlowPerSubscription.callPattern: 1 Retail Prices query per region and PostgreSQL meter group, with paging

triggerSignals: CPU, memory, storage, IOPS, or connection saturation
bloat, autovacuum, long transaction, or deadlock issue

calls: one metrics call per server with all required metric names
standardAssessment.recommendationDepth: compute right-size, storage, IOPS, connection, HA, replica, bloat, cost, security

calls: diagnostic logs, Query Store, or pg_stat_statements if available

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.p95CpuPct') lt 40 and get_value(resource, 'signals.p95MemoryPct') lt 60 and get_value(resource, 'signals.p95IopsConsumedPct') lt 40

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move to the cheapest valid compute SKU that satisfies CPU, memory, IOPS, connection, HA, and storage requirements with production buffer.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: p95CpuPct < 40 AND p95MemoryPct < 60 AND p95IopsConsumedPct < 40 for 14-30 days

recommendationRules.recommendation: Scale compute or tune workload/query patterns; choose the lowest-cost SKU that satisfies p99 demand plus production buffer.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.p95StoragePct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.p95IopsConsumedPct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.p95BandwidthConsumedPct') gte 80

recommendationRules.recommendation: Increase storage, change storage tier, tune indexes/queries, or reduce write amplification.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: p95StoragePct > 80 OR p95IopsConsumedPct > 80 OR p95BandwidthConsumedPct > 80 OR disk_queue_depth sustained

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable or tune connection pooling and reduce idle connections before scaling compute only for connections.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: p95ActiveConnectionsPct > 70 OR PgBouncer waiting client connections are high

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.bloatPercent') gte 20 or get_value(resource, 'signals.deadTupleCountHigh') eq True or get_value(resource, 'signals.longestTransactionTimeSec') gte 3600

recommendationRules.recommendation: Tune autovacuum, fix long-running transactions, vacuum/analyze affected databases, and review indexes.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: bloat_percent or n_dead_tup_user_tables is high OR longest_transaction_time_sec is high

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') eq True and get_value(resource, 'properties.highAvailabilityMode') eq 'Disabled'

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable zone-redundant or same-zone high availability based on RTO/RPO and regional support.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND highAvailability.mode is disabled

recommendationRules.recommendation: Investigate replica sizing, write volume, network/region distance, and read workload placement.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.steadyUsage') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.expectedLifetimeDays') gte 365

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate reserved capacity for stable baseline compute usage.
Use private access, private endpoints where applicable, and narrow firewall rules.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: publicNetworkAccess enabled or broad firewall rules exist

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresAzureADAuth') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.aadAuthEnabled') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable Microsoft Entra authentication and reduce password-based access where supported by applications.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: policy requires Microsoft Entra authentication and authConfig does not satisfy requirement

knownLimitations: Query-level tuning requires Query Store, pg_stat_statements, logs, or workload telemetry beyond platform metrics.
Retail prices are public prices and may not reflect negotiated rates or enterprise discounts; actual cost should come from Cost Management.
Changing SKU, storage tier, HA, networking, or authentication can require downtime, failover, maintenance windows, or application validation.
Bloat and autovacuum recommendations should be validated against database-specific workload behavior.

conditionLanguage.fieldPathRoot: normalized DB resource record passed to analyzer
conditionLanguage.unknownHandling: return investigate or cap classification; never mark stay/best from missing required data

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Current tier/size/configuration is acceptable; keep it and continue monitoring.
validWhen: required cost and metrics are present

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a smaller/lower/cooler/cheaper SKU, tier, capacity, retention, or feature set while preserving production constraints.
validWhen: paid capacity or premium feature is unused
target capability satisfies observed demand and policy

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a larger/higher/more resilient SKU, tier, capacity, throughput, or feature set because current configuration is constrained or risky.
validWhen: availability or security baseline requires a higher capability

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Stop, deallocate, archive, delete, expire, or remove the resource or child cost item after owner approval.
validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Do not resize automatically; collect missing data, resolve conflicting signals, or review owner/business context.

pythonAssessment.deterministicCasesSource: legacy scoring support; recommendation runtime should prefer assessmentRules/actionOutcomeRules evaluated against DB fields

runtimeContract.ruleSignalConvention: signals.rules.<recommendationRule.id> == true triggers that recommendation rule
collectorResponsibility: Collect Azure resource properties, Azure Monitor metrics, Cost Management rows, price cache, tags, policy, and advisor data outside recommendation runtime.
Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.
Calculate derived signals deterministically from stored DB values where raw metrics/properties need aggregation.
Do not require the analyzer to call Azure REST APIs while producing recommendations.
analyzerResponsibility: Load the resource assessment JSON by resourceType.
Load the normalized resource record from the database.
Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
